pi_items.source: ActivInspire documentation confirms .flipchart is standard extension (https://fileinfo.com/extension/flipchart). Task description line 5 specifies 'swot_template.flipchart'.
env.json defines user 'ga' with home directory /home/ga. Linux standard directory structure uses /home/username/Documents. Task description confirms this path in requirement 5.
Task description requirement 5: 'Save the flipchart to /home/ga/Documents/Flipcharts/swot_template.flipchart'. Combination of verified directory and filename.

Task description specifies: (1) title '1:1 Device Program SWOT' (requirement 2, contains 'Device Program'), (2) four quadrant labels: 'Strengths' (requirement 4, top-left), 'Weaknesses' (requirement 4, top-right), 'Opportunities' (requirement 4, bottom-left), 'Threats' (requirement 4, bottom-right).

Task requirement 3: 'Create a 2x2 grid structure using lines or rectangle shapes to divide the page into four quadrants.' A 2x2 grid requires minimum 2 graphic elements (one horizontal and one vertical line forming a cross). Verifier.py line 118 confirms: 'if graphic_elements >= 2'.

privileged_info_summary: The SWOT Analysis Template task is a user-generated flipchart creation task with no external data sources. ActivInspire is a real, well-documented educational software product by Promethean (https://www.prometheanworld.com/products/software/activinspire/). The task creates a 1-page flipchart file in .flipchart format saved to /home/ga/Documents/Flipcharts/swot_template.flipchart. The flipchart must contain the text 'Device Program', 'Strengths', 'Weaknesses', 'Opportunities', and 'Threats' arranged in a 2x2 grid layout created with a minimum of 2 graphic elements (lines or shapes). All metadata claims in task.json accurately reflect the task requirements specified in the task description.
